papers.title: Briefing chat: What Galileo’s scribbled margin notes reveal about his scientific journey
papers.authors: Benjamin Thompson, Maren Hunsberger
papers.summary: This article discusses Galileo's handwritten notes and their significance in understanding his scientific journey.

papers.title: Electric-vehicle batteries toughen up to beat the heat
papers.authors: Unknown authors
papers.summary: Researchers have developed electric-vehicle batteries that can withstand rising temperatures, improving their lifespan.

papers.title: Peanut-processing microbes ward off dangerous allergic shock
papers.authors: Unknown authors
papers.summary: Scientists have identified bacteria that can break down allergy-triggering compounds in peanuts, potentially reducing allergic reactions.

papers.title: Modelling the cosmos and imagining a future without meat: Books in brief
papers.authors: Andrew Robinson
papers.summary: This article reviews five science books, including one about modelling the cosmos and another about a future without meat.

papers.title: The missing pieces of menopause science
papers.authors: Linda Nordling
papers.summary: The article discusses the current state of menopause science, highlighting evidence gaps in treating perimenopause.

papers.title: What a viral TikTok taught me about personal storytelling in science
papers.authors: Jiun Youn
papers.summary: A viral TikTok video about personal storytelling in science made the author rethink the role of technology in teaching.

papers.title: Celebrate women in research and the networks that sustain them
papers.authors: Unknown authors
papers.summary: Nature celebrates women in research and the networks that support them on International Women's Day.

papers.title: Climate change is speeding up — the pace nearly doubled in ten years
papers.authors: Alexandra Witze
papers.summary: Climate change is accelerating at a rate of 0.35°C per decade, according to fresh analysis.

papers.title: Genetically modified pig liver keeps man alive until human organ transplant
papers.authors: Rachel Fieldhouse
papers.summary: A genetically modified pig liver was used to keep a man alive until a human organ transplant could be performed.

papers.title: Uncharted: Understanding women’s health across the body
papers.authors: Jyoti Madhusoodanan
papers.summary: Disaggregating health data by sex can help develop better diagnostics and treatments for women, but this is not being done enough.

papers.title: Cracked, but still there: the glass ceiling persists for senior women in science
papers.authors: Mangala Srinivas
papers.summary: A study highlights the persistence of gender-based bias in science, even for senior women.

papers.title: Author Correction: Programmable 200 GOPS Hopfield-inspired photonic Ising machine
papers.authors: Nayem Al-Kayed, Charles St-Arnault, Hugh Morison, A. Aadhi, Chaoran Huang et al. (+3 more)
papers.summary: A correction to a previous paper on a Hopfield-inspired photonic Ising machine.

papers.title: The first ‘AI societies’ are taking shape: how human-like are they?
papers.authors: Jenna Ahart
papers.summary: Scientists are studying 'social' interactions between artificial-intelligence agents.

papers.title: These brain cells clear proteins that contribute to Alzheimer’s
papers.authors: Rachel Fieldhouse
papers.summary: Specialized brain cells called tanycytes clear proteins that contribute to Alzheimer's disease.

papers.title: Daily briefing: How DNA testing can tell identical twins apart
papers.authors: Flora Graham
papers.summary: Emerging forensic methods can differentiate between identical twin suspects in criminal cases.

papers.title: The Epstein scandal is a wake-up call — new rules are needed on links with rich donors
papers.authors: Arthur Caplan
papers.summary: A scandal involving Jeffrey Epstein highlights the need for stronger governance in academia to prevent similar situations.

papers.title: Identical twins on trial: can DNA testing tell them apart?
papers.authors: Rachel Fieldhouse
papers.summary: New DNA analysis methods can help distinguish between identical twins in criminal trials.

papers.title: Inside Mexico’s stem-cell industry
papers.authors: Gemma Conroy
papers.summary: Researchers in Mexico are calling for stronger regulation of stem-cell treatments due to concerns over their safety and efficacy.

papers.title: How AI is shaping the war in Iran — and what’s next for future conflicts
papers.authors: Nicola Jones
papers.summary: The conflict in Iran is highlighting the use of artificial intelligence in warfare, with potential implications for future conflicts.

papers.title: Do obesity drugs treat addiction? Huge study hints at their promise
papers.authors: Mariana Lenharo
papers.summary: A large study of US military veterans suggests that anti-obesity medications may also help treat addiction.

papers.title: First ‘half Möbius’ carbon chain wows chemists
papers.authors: Davide Castelvecchi
papers.summary: Scientists have created a unique 'half Möbius' carbon chain with a looped shape, which could lead to new physical properties.

papers.title: High-rise transistors can be used to build space-saving circuits
papers.authors: Xiong Xiong, Yanqing Wu
papers.summary: Researchers have developed high-rise transistors that can be used to build compact electronic circuits, potentially leading to faster and more efficient devices.

papers.title: How these koalas bounced back from the brink of extinction
papers.authors: Mohana Basu
papers.summary: A study on koalas that were on the brink of extinction has found that tracking DNA recombination can help other species recover lost genetic diversity.

papers.title: Women in science are not a ‘problem to be fixed’
papers.authors: Holly Newson
papers.summary: A book review argues that women in science are not a 'problem to be fixed' and challenges false visions of gender equality in the workforce.

papers.title: Shared neural substrates of prosocial and parenting behaviours
papers.authors: Fangmiao Sun, Kayla Y. Lim, James Dang, Li I. Zhang, Ye Emily Wu et al. (+1 more)
papers.summary: Researchers have identified shared neural substrates of prosocial and parenting behaviors, which could have implications for understanding human behavior and development.

papers.title: Heart surgery with quick-setting magnetic fluid could prevent strokes
papers.authors: Nick Petrić Howe, Shamini Bundell
papers.summary: Researchers have developed a quick-setting magnetic fluid that can be injected into the heart to prevent strokes. Animal trials showed that the fluid safely fills the area where blood clots can form. This method could potentially reduce the risk of stroke.

papers.title: Magnetic gel injected into the heart could stop strokes
papers.authors: Nick Petrić Howe
papers.summary: A hydrogel-based plug injected into the heart has been shown to be effective in reducing the risk of stroke in rats and a pig. The plug blocks a part of the heart to prevent blood clots from forming.

papers.title: Genome modelling and design across all domains of life with Evo 2
papers.authors: Garyk Brixi, Matthew G. Durrant, Jerome Ku, Mohsen Naghipourfar, Michael Poli et al. (+57 more)
papers.summary: Evo 2 is an artificial intelligence-based biological foundation model that can predict functional properties from genomic sequences and provide a rich generative model for researchers in biology. It was trained on 9 trillion DNA base pairs spanning all domains of life.

papers.title: DICER cleavage fidelity is governed by 5′-end binding pockets
papers.authors: Minh Khoa Ngo, Cong Truc Le, Tuan Anh Nguyen
papers.summary: Researchers have shed light on the mechanism by which DICER, a key enzyme in the RNase III family, cleaves RNA at precise locations to produce small RNAs. Cryo-electron microscopy and massively parallel assays were used to study this process.

papers.title: Limited thermal tolerance in tropical insects and its genomic signature
papers.authors: Kim L. Holzmann, Thomas Schmitzer, Antonia Abels, Marko Čorkalo, Oliver Mitesser et al. (+13 more)
papers.summary: A survey of tropical insect populations found that species from lowland areas have low capacity to survive increased temperatures. Thermal tolerance is limited by fundamental properties of protein architecture.

papers.title: A metabolic alarmin from keratinocytes potentiates systemic humoral immunity
papers.authors: Zhenglin Ji, Ji Gao, Shaocun Zhang, Jiajie Li, Haijing Wu et al. (+39 more)
papers.summary: A metabolic intermediate called farnesyl pyrophosphate acts as an alarm signal to enhance the body's immune response by stimulating the production of certain proteins in skin cells.

papers.title: Long-term thrombus-free left atrial appendage occlusion via magnetofluids
papers.authors: Shu Wang, Wenhao Ju, Donglin Zhuang, Zhecheng Chen, Dongliang Zhao et al. (+11 more)
papers.summary: Researchers have developed a new method for blocking blood clots in the heart using magnetized fluids that can withstand high-speed blood flow.

papers.title: Lipid metabolism drives dietary effects on T cell ferroptosis and immunity
papers.authors: Naiqi Wang, Zhian Chen, Yin Yao, Chenglong Sun, Wei Wei et al. (+35 more)
papers.summary: Dietary composition affects the function of immune cells called T cells by influencing the balance of fatty acids in the body, which can lead to cell death.

papers.title: Merlin: a computed tomography vision–language foundation model and dataset
papers.authors: Louis Blankemeier, Ashwin Kumar, Joseph Paul Cohen, Jiaming Liu, Longchao Liu et al. (+35 more)
papers.summary: A new AI model called Merlin has been developed to analyze medical images and patient data, demonstrating strong performance in various medical tasks.

papers.title: Microbiota-mediated induction of beige adipocytes in response to dietary cues
papers.authors: Takeshi Tanoue, Manabu Nagayama, Ayumi J. A. Roochana, Samuel Zimmerman, Orr Ashenberg et al. (+28 more)
papers.summary: A low-protein diet can trigger the growth of brown fat cells in mice by altering the balance of gut microorganisms, which can help the body respond to dietary cues.

papers.title: Mechanism of co-transcriptional cap snatching by influenza polymerase
papers.authors: Alexander Helmut Rotsch, Delong Li, Maud Dupont, Tim Krischuns, Ute Neef et al. (+9 more)
papers.summary: Researchers studied how the influenza virus polymerase snatches RNA caps from host cells during transcription, revealing the molecular mechanisms behind this process.

papers.title: Cell-free chromatin state tracing reveals disease origin and therapy responses
papers.authors: Xubin Chen, Xiaoxuan Meng, Weilong Zhang, Xiawei Zhang, Yaping Zhang et al. (+26 more)
papers.summary: Scientists developed a method to track chromatin states in cell-free DNA from human plasma, enabling the identification of disease origins and therapy responses.

papers.title: The oldest articulated bony fish from the early Silurian period
papers.authors: You-An Zhu, Yang Chen, Qiang Li, Wen-Jin Zhao, Zheng-Da Zhou et al. (+7 more)
papers.summary: Fossil discovery reveals the oldest articulated bony fish from the early Silurian period, providing new insights into the evolution of fish.

papers.title: Bulk hexagonal diamond
papers.authors: Shoulong Lai, Xigui Yang, Jiuyang Shi, Shijie Liu, Ying Guo et al. (+7 more)
papers.summary: Researchers synthesized millimetre-sized phase-pure hexagonal diamond, a new polymorph of cubic diamond, by compressing graphite under high pressures and temperatures.

papers.title: Sea level much higher than assumed in most coastal hazard assessments
papers.authors: Katharina Seeger, Philip S. J. Minderhoud
papers.summary: A global meta-analysis shows that measured coastal mean sea level is higher than assumed in most coastal hazard assessments, with implications for coastal hazard planning.

papers.title: A glucocorticoid–FAS axis controls immune evasion during metastatic seeding
papers.authors: Monica Cassandras, Xavier Sanchez, Lauren Hsu, Yu Huang, Adam J. Getzler et al. (+17 more)
papers.summary: Researchers found that the glucocorticoid receptor plays a key role in helping triple-negative breast cancer cells evade the immune system during the initial stages of metastasis.

papers.title: The molecular basis of force selectivity by PIEZO2
papers.authors: Eric M. Mulhall, Oleg Yarishkin, Rose Z. Hill, Anna K. Koster, Ardem Patapoutian
papers.summary: The study reveals that PIEZO2 channels respond differently to mechanical stimuli compared to PIEZO1 channels, and that PIEZO2 is more rigid than PIEZO1.

papers.title: Advancing operational global aerosol forecasting with machine learning
papers.authors: Ke Gui, Xutao Zhang, Huizheng Che, Lei Li, Yu Zheng et al. (+17 more)
papers.summary: A machine-learning-driven forecasting system was able to produce reliable 5-day forecasts of aerosol optical components and surface concentrations in just 1 minute.

papers.title: Wind shear enhances soil moisture influence on rapid thunderstorm growth
papers.authors: Christopher M. Taylor, Cornelia Klein, Emma J. Barton, Sebastian Hahn, Wolfgang Wagner
papers.summary: Researchers found that wind shear amplifies the impact of soil moisture in triggering rapidly developing thunderstorms in sub-Saharan Africa.

papers.title: Structural basis of RNA-guided transcription by a dCas12f–σ<sup>E</sup>–RNAP complex
papers.authors: Renjian Xiao, Florian T. Hoffmann, Dan Xie, Tanner Wiegand, Adriana I. Palmieri et al. (+2 more)
papers.summary: The study reveals the structural basis of RNA-guided transcription by a dCas12f–σE–RNAP complex, which involves the recruitment of RNAP to targeted DNA and the stabilization of the −10 element.

papers.title: Homologous recombination deficiency and hemizygosity drive resistance in breast cancer
papers.authors: Anton Safonov, Minna Lee, David N. Brown, Luca Boscolo Bielo, Miika Mehine et al. (+52 more)
papers.summary: Researchers identified genomic patterns in breast cancer that drive resistance to therapy. These patterns are caused by interactions between germline and somatic mutations. This finding could lead to new treatments for breast cancer.

papers.title: Exapted CRISPR–Cas12f homologues drive RNA-guided transcription
papers.authors: Florian T. Hoffmann, Tanner Wiegand, Adriana I. Palmieri, Juniper Glass-Klaiber, Renjian Xiao et al. (+6 more)
papers.summary: Scientists discovered a new way to control gene expression using CRISPR-Cas12f. This system uses specialized proteins to activate genes without needing specific promoter motifs.

papers.title: Wide-swath altimetry maps bank shapes and storage changes in global rivers
papers.authors: A. Cerbelaud, J. Wade, C. H. David, M. Durand, R. P. M. Frasson et al. (+2 more)
papers.summary: Researchers used satellite data to map the shapes and storage changes of rivers worldwide. This study provides new insights into river dynamics and water storage.

papers.title: Largest Silurian fish illuminates the origin of osteichthyan characters
papers.authors: Jing Lu, Brian Choo, Wenjin Zhao, You-an Zhu, Xindong Cui et al. (+9 more)
papers.summary: A new fossil discovery sheds light on the evolution of osteichthyan characters. The fossil, Megamastax amblyodus, provides new information about the morphology of ancient fish.

papers.title: Precancerous niche remodelling dictates nascent tumour persistence
papers.authors: G. Skrupskelyte, J. E. Rojo Arias, H. Ajith, Y. Dang, D. Rossetti et al. (+16 more)
papers.summary: Researchers found that a specific niche in the body can support the persistence of nascent tumors. This study provides new insights into the early stages of cancer development.

papers.title: Rising temperatures pose a threat to tropical insects
papers.authors: Vojtech Novotny
papers.summary: A study found that rising temperatures pose a threat to tropical insects, potentially leading to biodiversity loss in lowland areas.

papers.title: Fresh claim of making elusive ‘hexagonal’ diamond is the strongest yet
papers.authors: Mark Peplow
papers.summary: Researchers claim to have found evidence of a rare form of diamond, sparking debate and further research.

papers.title: Stress-hormone signalling protects spreading cancer cells from immune system
papers.authors: Unknown authors
papers.summary: Cancer cells can evade the immune system by harnessing stress-hormone signaling, suggesting a potential therapeutic target for preventing metastasis.

papers.title: AI can write genomes — how long until it creates synthetic life?
papers.authors: Ewen Callaway
papers.summary: An AI model can generate short genome sequences, but further advances are needed to create synthetic life.

papers.title: Magnetic fluid offers better seal in heart-plugging medical procedure
papers.authors: Yu Shrike Zhang
papers.summary: A magnetic fluid has been used to close off part of the heart in animals, potentially improving a procedure that prevents stroke in people with irregular heartbeats.

papers.title: Funding from individual donors: lessons from the Epstein case
papers.authors: Unknown authors
papers.summary: Universities need to establish and empower compliance teams to ensure adherence to ethical funding policies.

papers.title: A genetic switch turns off parental behaviour and drives infanticide in male striped mice
papers.authors: Unknown authors
papers.summary: The brain and behavior of the African striped mouse reveal that social environment and gene expression affect male parenting.

papers.title: Do wet or dry soils trigger thunderstorms? It depends on how the wind blows
papers.authors: Craig R. Ferguson
papers.summary: Wind conditions affect the connection between soil moisture and thunderstorms, which could be used to inform forecasting.

papers.title: Climate change and geopolitics threaten water supplies — but disaster is not inevitable
papers.authors: Ali Tauqeer Sheikh
papers.summary: The Indus Waters Treaty withstood several armed conflicts and a huge loss of glaciers, and could serve as a blueprint for others.

papers.title: Cancer blood tests are everywhere. Do they really work?
papers.authors: Nic Fleming
papers.summary: Cancer blood tests claim to detect dozens of cancer types, but some scientists say they could be missing many cancers or delivering the wrong diagnosis.

papers.title: Skin cells boost distant antibody responses
papers.authors: Xueping Zhu, Caroline L. Sokol
papers.summary: Skin cells have been found to play a key role in boosting the production of antibodies to protect the whole body.

papers.title: How a mathematician is cracking open Mexico’s powerful drug cartels
papers.authors: Gemma Conroy
papers.summary: A mathematician has developed models of organized crime that could improve public safety in Mexico.

papers.title: Under pressure: the reality of Mexico’s research system
papers.authors: Oscar Xavier Guerrero Gutiérrez
papers.summary: Mexican scientists face unstable conditions, including limited funding and resources, which can hinder their research.

papers.title: Radiology AI makes consistent diagnoses using 3D images from different health centres
papers.authors: Unknown authors
papers.summary: A 3D vision-language model called Merlin has been trained to make consistent diagnoses using 3D images from different health centers.

papers.title: Daily briefing: The return of the snail — the month’s best science images
papers.authors: Flora Graham
papers.summary: This article features a collection of science images from February, including a snail, and discusses the potential of cancer blood tests and the impact of losing visibility of the stars.

papers.title: Hunt for reactive metabolites uncovers unusual chemistry in a human pathogen
papers.authors: Unknown authors
papers.summary: A new workflow was developed to capture reactive compounds produced by a bacterium that causes lung disease, leading to the discovery of two unusual molecules with potential broad synthetic applications.

papers.title: Author Correction: Healthy forests safeguard traditional wild meat food systems in Amazonia
papers.authors: André Pinassi Antunes, Pedro de Araujo Lima Constantino, Julia E. Fa, Daniel P. Munari, Thais Q. Morcatty et al. (+53 more)
papers.summary: A correction to a previous paper on the importance of healthy forests in safeguarding traditional wild meat food systems in Amazonia.

papers.title: Genetically encoded assembly recorder temporally resolves cellular history
papers.authors: Yuqing Yan, Jiaxi Lu, Zhe Li, Zuohan Zhao, Timothy F. Shay et al. (+11 more)
papers.summary: A genetically encoded assembly recorder was developed to temporally resolve cellular history, providing new insights into cellular processes.

papers.title: Jam-packed star system is most compact of its kind ever found
papers.authors: Unknown authors
papers.summary: A star system consisting of four Sun-like stars was found to be the most compact of its kind, with all four stars fitting within the area of Jupiter's orbit.

papers.title: Releasing open-weight AI in steps would alleviate risks
papers.authors: S. M. Nuruzzaman Nobel, Maxine Tan
papers.summary: Releasing open-weight AI in steps could alleviate risks associated with its development and deployment.

papers.title: From the first telephone to videoconferencing in 100 years
papers.authors: Unknown authors
papers.summary: This paper discusses the history of telecommunications, from the first telephone to videoconferencing, highlighting the rapid progress made in the past 100 years.

papers.title: South Korea’s AI framework act focuses on rights and safety
papers.authors: WooJung Jon
papers.summary: South Korea's AI framework act prioritizes rights and safety, focusing on responsible AI development.

papers.title: Climate research is global — risks and responsibilities should also be distributed
papers.authors: Zhenhua Rui, Lu Lin, Hongfang Lu, Y. Frank Cheng
papers.summary: Climate research is a global effort, and risks and responsibilities should be distributed accordingly, rather than being concentrated in a few countries.

papers.title: Why ‘quantum proteins’ could be the next big thing in biology
papers.authors: Nicola Jones
papers.summary: Researchers propose the development of 'quantum proteins' that could offer unprecedented views inside cells, potentially revolutionizing biology.

papers.title: Dynamics of natural selection preceding human viral epidemics and pandemics
papers.authors: Jennifer L. Havens, Sergei L. Kosakovsky Pond, Jordan D. Zehr, Jonathan E. Pekar, Edyth Parker, Michael Worobey, Kristian G. Andersen, Joel O. Wertheim
papers.summary: A study of viral evolution reveals that adaptation in the host reservoir is not necessary for novel zoonotic virus epidemics and pandemics, including SARS-CoV-2.

papers.title: β-hydroxybutyrate enhances the metabolic fitness of CAR T cells in cancer
papers.authors: Shan Liu, Puneeth Guruprasad, Ranjani Ramasubramanian, Bhoomi Madhu, Luca Paruzzo, Kecheng Han, Andre Kelly, Alexander Shestov, He N. Xu, Alberto Carturan, Chaoting Zhou, Kevin R. Amses, Amichay Afriat, Lev Litichevskiy, Jason Lin, Ezra Dubowitz, Neil Tangal, Jing Zhang, Alana McSween, Melody Tan, Federico Stella, Andrew Lee, Siena Nason, Xianxin Hua, Michael Schneider, Madeleine Sleeman, Yunlin Zhang, Giulia Gabrielli, Ziqi Yang, Raymone Pajarillo, Ruchi Patel, Guido Ghilardi, Vrutti Patel, Akshita Joshi, Shunzhou Jiang, Yanqing Jiang, Patrizia Porazzi, Julia C. Tchou, Brian Keith, Mingyao Li, Elise Chong, Stephen J. Schuster, Michael Milone, Joshua Rabinowitz, Roddy S. O’Connor, Christoph A. Thaiss, Maayan Levy, Marco Ruella
papers.summary: β-hydroxybutyrate (BHB) supplementation enhances the metabolic fitness of CAR T cells, leading to improved proliferation, cytokine production, and tumor control.

papers.title: Light-directed evolution of dynamic, multi-state, and computational protein functionalities
papers.authors: Vojislav Gligorovski, Marco Labagnara, Lorenzo Scutteri, Marius Blackholm, Andreas Möglich, Nahal Mansouri, Sahand Jamal Rahi
papers.summary: Optovolution is a method that leverages optogenetics and yeast cell cycles to evolve complex protein behaviors, such as light-responsive regulators and logic gates.

papers.title: Selective targeting of endothelial and perivascular angiocrine ROCK2 treats liver fibrosis
papers.authors: Yan Hu, Biao Yang, Chengju Xiao, Xinchun Yang, Hua Zhang, Pengbo Yang, Xiao Du, Guixiang Zhang, Beibei Liang, Nan Bai, Daoguang Zhang, Dongbo Wu, Qinghua Luo, Yangjing Teng, Yumeng Chen, Zhiqing Guo, Chengjian Zhao, Tinghong Ye, Dong Chai, Xiaolong Qi, Weiting Zhong, Jie Chen, Haohao Dong, Jinhang Gao, Huanhuan He, Junlei Chang, Xuri Li, Liang Peng, Shahin Rafii, Scott L. Friedman, Cheng Yi, Yun Cai, Yanping Zhao, Hongjun Wang, Chen Wang, Zhongwei Cao, Bi-Sen Ding
papers.summary: Targeting ROCK2 in endothelial and perivascular cells with a selective inhibitor, TDI01, shows promise in treating liver fibrosis in preclinical models and human patients.

papers.title: Deltaviruses spread through a viral Trojan Horse
papers.authors: Joe McKellar, Aurélien Fouillen, Sébastien Lyonnais, Marie-Pierre Blanchard, Florian Seigneuret, Antonio Trullo, Zoé Denis, Roni Sleiman, Brandon Le Bon, Laurence Berry, Lorena Kumarasinghe, Jules Bouget, Arinya Apostel, Maria Bacia-Verloop, Sylvain De Rossi, Yasmine Messaoud-Nacer, Sophie Colomb, Solange Desagher, Isabelle K. Vila, Nadine Laguette, Sébastien Granier, Hugues de Rocquigny, Raphael Gaudin, Valérie Courgnaud, Irina Gutsche, Jussi Hepojoki, Karim Majzoub
papers.summary: Satellite viruses, or deltaviruses, can spread by hiding within helper virus particles, acting as a 'Trojan Horse' to infect new species and organs, including the human brain.

papers.title: Metabolite-gated vascular contractility switch: OXGR1 activation mechanism enables agonist therapy for rosacea erythema
papers.authors: Wenqin Xiao, Yan Zhu, Xinjie Tang, Kongkai Zhu, Weifeng Zhang, Mengting Chen, Kui Cai, San Xu, Zheng Wu, Mei Wang, Jiayi Liu, Linglong Long, Zixin Tan, Aike Wu, Songqi Zhou, Zhixiang Zhao, Yan Tang, Yingxue Huang, Ben Wang, Fangfen Liu, Qian Wang, Fan Yang, Dan Jian, Wei Shi, Hongfu Xie, Xiang Chen, Lulu Guo, Zhili Deng, Jinpeng Sun, Ji Li
papers.summary: A metabolite, α-KG, activates the OXGR1-Gq-MYL9 axis in vascular smooth muscle cells, leading to a contractility switch that alleviates erythema in rosacea-like models.

papers.title: Genetically encoded fluorescent reporters to visualize α-synuclein pathology in live brain
papers.authors: Li Zhang, Minhui Yu, Guoqing Chen, Siyuan Ge, Mengdi Wang, Xianying Zhang, Miao Zhao, Huating Gu, Meizhu Huang, Aixue Liu, Gengxin Ran, Zeyuan Liu, Tiepeng Liao, Qi Chen, Chenjian Miao, Yao Lu, Yibing Wang, Fengchao Wang, Zhihui Liu, Hongying Zhu, Qian Wu, Zhuan Zhou, Wei Xiong, Xiaoqun Wang, Peng Cao
papers.summary: Researchers developed genetically encoded fluorescent reporters to visualize α-synuclein pathology in live brain cells. This allows for the study of the propagation and pathological effects of α-synuclein inclusions. The reporters were created by introducing specific genes into mouse lines, enabling diverse applications in studying α-synuclein.

papers.title: Efficient amyloid-β degradation in Alzheimer’s disease using SPYTACs
papers.authors: Fei Teng, Jing Liu, Tongtong Cui, Xiangtian Tan, Kailun Liu, Zongren Hou, Li Zhou, Yuanzhi Xie, Rongqi Li, Da Li, Bojin Li, Dongmei Wang, Qi Zhou, Baoyang Hu, Wei Li
papers.summary: A synthetic peptide-programmed targeted protein degradation platform called SPYTAC was used to efficiently degrade amyloid-β in the brain and periphery. This led to improved cognition, reduced neuroinflammation, and microhemorrhage in mice with Alzheimer's disease.

papers.title: Restoring circadian rhythms in the hypothalamic paraventricular nucleus reverses aging biomarkers and extends lifespan in male mice
papers.authors: Haijiao Zhao, Meimei Liao, Ran Huo, Ting He, Hongni Tian, Zeqi Li, Chen Chen, Ziqing Yu, Juan Chai, Xiaocui Song, Ruichao Shao, Shuhua Ying, Wen Gao, Ling Liu, Di Sang, Qi Li, Haohong Li, Fengchao Wang, Dapeng Ju, Eric Erquan Zhang
papers.summary: Researchers found that enhancing circadian amplitude in the hypothalamic paraventricular nucleus neurons of mice alleviated age-related pathologies and extended lifespan. This was achieved through treatment with 3′-deoxyadenosine.

papers.title: Mettl3 promotes reprogramming and axonogenesis of induced retinal ganglion cells
papers.authors: Ke Zhang, Huilin Liang, Shuyi Chen
papers.summary: Mettl3 was found to promote reprogramming and axonogenesis of induced retinal ganglion cells through m6A modification. This modification drives the degradation of modified transcripts, accelerating neural fate reprogramming and axonogenesis.

papers.title: Genome-edited safe and immune-evasive human pluripotent cells: Potential solution for allogeneic therapies
papers.authors: Vivian Tam, Nicole Ching Man Wong, Andrew Chung Hin Poon, Mengxia Zhu, Ting Mei, Janette Kwok, Patrick Chu, Eric D. Jong, Jean Kit Tang, Andras Nagy, Danny Chan
papers.summary: Researchers created immune-evasive human pluripotent cells using combined SafeCell and AlloAccept engineered technologies. These cells were shown to be accepted by the immune system in humanized mice, even in pre-sensitized allogeneic recipients, promising a safe and universal source of cells for therapeutic applications.

papers.title: Delayed forebrain excitatory and inhibitory neurogenesis in STRADA-related megalencephaly via mTOR hyperactivity
papers.authors: Tong Pan, Grace Lin, Xuan Li, Debora VanHeyningen, John C. Walker, Sahej Kohli, Aiswarya Saravanan, Amrita Kondur, Daniel C. Jaklic, Saul Pantoja-Gutierrez, Shivanshi Vaid, Julie Sturza, Ken Inoki, Tomozumi Imamichi, Weizhong Chang, Louis T. Dang
papers.summary: Researchers found that STRADA loss leads to mTOR hyperactivity, causing delayed neurogenesis in human forebrain organoids. This was associated with altered interneuron development and metabolic pathways. mTOR inhibition rescued most changes, suggesting potential therapeutic strategies for STRADA-related disease.

papers.title: Establishing mouse forebrain organoids as models of intrinsic cortical network assembly
papers.authors: Sebastian Hernandez, Hunter E. Schweiger, Isabel Cline, Gregory A. Kaurala, Ash Robbins, Daniel Solis, Samira Vera-Choqqueccota, Jinghui Geng, Tjitse van der Molen, Francisco Reyes, Chinweike Norman Asogwa, Kateryna Voitiuk, Mattia Chini, Marco Rolandi, Sofie R. Salama, Bradley M. Colquitt, Tal Sharf, David Haussler, Mircea Teodorescu, Mohammed A. Mostajo-Radji
papers.summary: Mouse forebrain organoids were used to model intrinsic cortical network assembly. The organoids self-organized into small-world neural networks, with dorsal organoids showing progressive synchronization and ventral organoids developing enhanced clustering and modularity.

papers.title: Genomic characterization of sub-populations in human pluripotent stem cell-derived retinal progenitor cells driving retinal lamination
papers.authors: Yasuaki Iwama, Tomohiro Masuda, Mika Yoshimura, Mikiya Watanabe, Yoko Ohigashi, Martin Friedlander, Kohji Nishida, Masayo Takahashi, Itoshi Nikaido, Michiko Mandai
papers.summary: Researchers characterized sub-populations in human pluripotent stem cell-derived retinal progenitor cells driving retinal lamination. Prolonged proliferation with sequential canonical and non-canonical WNT signaling was associated with retinal laminar structures.

papers.title: Selective Cav1.3 inhibition promotes survival of transplanted dopaminergic neurons via the CaMKII-p65-p53 pathway
papers.authors: Qingyuan Y. Tang, Jing Sun, Jun Xue, Chen Qiu, Tian Wang, Tatyana Matveeva, Yifan F. Wu, Xiaoying Y. Li, Tianlin L. Cheng, Hyemyung Seo, Mingtao T. Li, Shanshan S. Ma, Jeffrey S. Schweitzer, Bin Song
papers.summary: Inhibiting the calcium channel Cav1.3 in stem cell-derived dopamine neurons protected them from inflammatory and toxic damage by blocking the CaMKII-p65-p53 pathway. This strategy significantly improved transplanted mDAN survival.

papers.title: Messenger RNA delivery into Sertoli cells restores fertility to congenitally infertile male mice
papers.authors: Mito Kanatsu-Shinohara, Narumi Ogonuki, Shogo Matoba, Takehiro Miyazaki, Hiroko Morimoto, Tianjiao Liu, Naoto Yoshinaga, Atsuo Ogura, Satoshi Uchida, Takashi Shinohara
papers.summary: Researchers developed a method to deliver mRNA into Sertoli cells, restoring fertility to congenitally infertile male mice. Delivery of wild-type Cldn11 mRNA restored haploid round spermatids, allowing offspring to be obtained by spermatid microinjection.
